questions.question: A process where urine is removed from the body is called __
questions.explanation: A process where urine is removed from the body is called Micturition.

questions.question: What results in severe, acute, or incurable liver disease?

options.label: C
options.text: Elimination of water, drugs, and toxins from the body

questions.explanation: Blood clotting, Production of blood proteins, and Elimination of water, drugs, and toxins from the body are results in severe, acute, or incurable liver disease.

questions.question: Which of the following gland of the body produces antibodies?
questions.explanation: The thymus gland secretes thymosin, which matures lymphocytes into T-lymphocytes which produces antibodies and thereby increases immunity.

questions.question: Malaria is an acute febrile illness caused by__.
questions.explanation: Malaria is an acute febrile illness caused by Plasmodium parasites. Malaria is a parasitic disease that is transmitted to humans through the bite of infected mosquitoes.

questions.question: Which of the following is an important objective of biotechnology in the field of agriculture?

options.label: B
options.text: to produce pest-resistant varieties of plants

options.label: D
options.text: to reduce the number of plants

answer.label: B
answer.text: to produce pest-resistant varieties of plants

questions.explanation: An important objective of biotechnology in the field of agriculture is to produce pest-resistant varieties of plants.

questions.question: What is Bt cotton?
questions.explanation: Bt cotton is a transgenic plant. Plants in which one or more genes of another species have been introduced into the genome using genetic engineering procedures are called transgenic plants.

questions.question: Which of the following are examples of hydrophilic molecules?
questions.explanation: A molecule whose interaction with water and other polar substances is more thermodynamically favorable than its interaction with oil or other hydrophobic solvents is called a hydrophilic molecule. Sugar, salt, starch, and cellulose are examples of hydrophilic molecules.

questions.question: Jellyfish are an example of which type of phylum?
questions.explanation: Cnidarian, also called coelenterate, any member of the phylum Cnidaria (Coelenterata), a group made up of more than 9,000 living species. ? Mostly marine animals, the cnidarians include the corals, hydras, jellyfish, Portuguese men-of-war, sea anemones, sea pens, sea whips, and sea fans.

questions.question: The largest White Blood Corpuscle is ____:
questions.explanation: Monocytes are the largest white blood cell about 20m in diameter. When they leave the blood they’re called macrophages. Macrophages work with lymphocytes as antigen presenting cells.

questions.question: Which of the following plant pigments absorbs in red and far red region of light?
questions.explanation: Phytochrome is a photoreceptor, a pigment that plants use to detect light. It is a protein with a bilin chromophore. It detects mainly red and far-red region of the visible spectrum and regulates germination of seeds in photoblastic seeds.
